root_text.an10.9:1.1: “Saddho ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti, no ca sīlavā …pe…
root_text.an10.9:1.5: parisāvacaro ca, no ca visārado parisāya dhammaṁ deseti …
root_text.an10.9:1.6: visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseti, no ca vinayadharo …
root_text.an10.9:1.7: vinayadharo ca, no ca āraññiko pantasenāsano …
root_text.an10.9:1.8: āraññiko ca pantasenāsano, no ca ye te santā vimokkhā atikkamma rūpe āruppā te kāyena phusitvā viharati …
root_text.an10.9:1.9: ye te santā vimokkhā atikkamma rūpe āruppā te ca kāyena phusitvā viharati, no ca āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an10.9:1.12: ‘kintāhaṁ saddho ca assaṁ, sīlavā ca, bahussuto ca, dhammakathiko ca, parisāvacaro ca, visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseyyaṁ, vinayadharo ca, āraññiko ca pantasenāsano, ye te santā vimokkhā atikkamma rūpe āruppā te ca kāyena phusitvā vihareyyaṁ, āsavānañca khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an10.9:2.1: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddho ca hoti, sīlavā ca, bahussuto ca, dhammakathiko ca, parisāvacaro ca, visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseti, vinayadharo ca, āraññiko ca pantasenāsano, ye te santā vimokkhā atikkamma rūpe āruppā te ca kāyena phusitvā viharati, āsavānañca khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati;
root_text.an10.9:2.3: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dasahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu samantapāsādiko ca hoti sabbākāraparipūro cā”ti.

translation_text.an10.9:0.3: Inspiring All Around: the Peaceful Liberations
translation_text.an10.9:1.1: “A mendicant is faithful, but not ethical. …
translation_text.an10.9:1.5: they don’t teach Dhamma to the assembly with assurance …
translation_text.an10.9:1.6: they’re not an expert in the training …
translation_text.an10.9:1.7: they don’t stay in the wilderness, in remote lodgings …
translation_text.an10.9:1.8: they don’t have direct meditative experience of the peaceful liberations that are formless, transcending form …
translation_text.an10.9:1.9: they don’t realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an10.9:1.10: So they’re incomplete in that respect,
translation_text.an10.9:1.12: ‘How can I become faithful, ethical, and learned, a Dhamma speaker, one who frequents assemblies, one who teaches Dhamma to the assembly with assurance, an expert in the training, one who lives in the wilderness, in remote lodgings, one who gets the formless liberations, and one who lives having realized the ending of defilements?’
translation_text.an10.9:2.1: When they’re faithful, ethical, and learned, a Dhamma speaker, one who frequents assemblies, one who teaches Dhamma to the assembly with assurance, an expert in the monastic law, one who lives in the wilderness, in remote lodgings, one who gets the formless liberations, and one who lives having realized the ending of defilements,
translation_text.an10.9:2.3: A mendicant who possesses these ten qualities is impressive all around, and is complete in every respect.”
